description: Development log for the Google Fonts reproducible build system. Posts are in reverse chronological order.

posts.title: Build failure investigation begins: 471 → 470 failures
posts.body:
With all 1,266 buildable families tested (1,258 successfully, 8 unreachable), we turned attention to the 471 build failures. All failures were categorized into 14 root causes:

- **fontprimer-missing** (110): The `fontprimer` recipe provider wasn't installed. Fixed by installing it, but most families hit secondary errors (missing `pyftfeatfreeze`, missing generated guide files for the Playwrite monorepo).
- **instance-ufo-naming** (93): fontmake/glyphsLib version mismatch — instance UFOs generated with different naming than expected.
- **gftools-fontsetter-bug** (20): `gftools-fontsetter` receives wrong arguments — a pre-existing gftools bug.
- **fontmake errors** (94): Various compilation failures (source issues, version incompatibilities).
- **Other** (154): master incompatibility, legacy formats, missing sources, etc.

The build registry now records `failure_category` and `failure_message` for every failing family, plus `build_environment`, `source_format`, and `config_yaml_source` for all families.

Also added a TTS announcer (using piper) that speaks build results aloud as they happen — surprisingly motivating when you hear "3 new families building correctly! 63.2 percent building."

**Tools installed this session:** fontprimer 0.2.2, paintcompiler 0.3.4, opentype-feature-freezer 1.32.2.

**Bug fixed:** fontprimer 0.2.2 crashes with babelfont 3.1.3 because `Instance.styleName` was renamed to `Instance.name`. Patched locally.

posts.title: Reproducible build testing reaches 100%: 1,258 of 1,266 families
posts.body:
Completed testing all 1,266 buildable families in the Google Fonts library. Results:

| Status | Count | % |
|--------|-------|---|
| Byte-identical | 110 | 8.7% |
| Compiler version diff | 652 | 51.8% |
| Build failure | 471 | 37.4% |
| Timestamp diff | 13 | 1.0% |
| Name table diff | 8 | 0.6% |
| Metadata stanza wrong | 4 | 0.3% |

784 families have deep analysis (root cause breakdown, reflow risk assessment). Key findings:
- 265 font files have metadata-only differences (functionally reproducible)
- 754 font files have zero reflow risk
- Only 1 family (Artifika) has genuine reflow risk from advance width changes

The dashboard now has dedicated pages for Build System, Build Dependencies, Build Timings, and Build Failures — all with mandatory post-batch sync.

posts.title: Dashboard expansion: dependencies, timings, and strict sync policy
posts.body:
Added three new dashboard pages:

1. **Build Dependencies** — cross-reference of all dependencies (source formats, ttfautohint versions, build steps, prebuild tools) with per-family listings. Category filter and family search.
2. **Build Timings** — build time per family with histogram, median/P95/max stats. Multiple data points preserved across rebuilds for trend analysis.
3. **Build Failures** — categorized failures with 3-phase fix plan and progress tracking over time.

Established strict policy: the build registry is a permanent record of everything needed to rebuild the full library. Every family must have `source_format`, `config_yaml_source`, `build_environment`, and (if failing) `failure_category` recorded. All data timestamped for historical tracking.

`build_dependencies.json` moved to google/fonts `build_tools/` (canonical source) with synced copy for dashboard.

posts.title: Project inception: validating METADATA.pb source stanzas at scale
posts.body:
Built a system to validate that `METADATA.pb` source stanzas (repository_url, commit) in google/fonts are correct by actually building fonts from upstream sources and comparing them to shipped binaries.

The pipeline: download source snapshot → run prebuild commands → resolve config.yaml → run gftools-builder → compare via SHA256 + TTX diff → deep analysis (ttfautohint, per-glyph coordinates, advance widths, line metrics) → categorize.

Key infrastructure challenges solved:
- **virtiofs FD accumulation**: virtiofsd leaks file descriptors during builds. Mitigated by dropping VFS caches every 5 families.
- **Download timeouts**: 5-minute SIGALRM prevents stalls on unreachable repos.
- **Build output discovery**: Extended `find_built_font()` to handle bracket characters in filenames, search parent directories, and exclude reference font directories.
- **Prebuild support**: Auto-detection of Makefile/build.sh/build.py, plus explicit prebuild commands in registry (glyphs2ufo, custom scripts).
